response.justification: While Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with strong fundamentals in testing and software craftsmanship, he fundamentally mismatches the role's core requirements. The position demands 8 years of C++ experience; his resume shows ~6 months (National Instruments, 2014-2015) and no recent work in the language. He lacks demonstrated technical leadership experience mentoring infrastructure/platform teams and has not led large-scale cloud compute platform projects. His career trajectory is toward full-stack generalism and AI tooling, not infrastructure specialization.
strengths: Strong software engineering fundamentals with proven commitment to TDD, testing practices, and code quality across multiple roles
Relevant experience with distributed systems at Dunnhumby (micro-services handling millions of requests/second) and AWS CDK infrastructure-as-code at Meta
Demonstrated ability to launch and maintain production software, establish development practices, and create training/mentoring content
concerns: Critical gap: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience (2014-2015) versus 8-year minimum requirement; primary languages are JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang
No documented technical leadership or mentoring experience in cloud/DevOps roles; career lacks formal people management or team leadership positions
Limited production-level cloud architecture experience; background is backend/full-stack services, not cloud platform or compute infrastructure design

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise depth (8yr minimum vs ~6mo actual)
Technical leadership experience in cloud/infrastructure domain
Production distributed systems and infrastructure background
